mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 10 percent, severe labor market deterioration signals a deep recession that suppresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 10.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with falling interest rates creates a liquidity-driven consumption boom that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and falling FEDFUNDS yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum that triggers immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260, accelerating inflation forces monetary tightening expectations that compress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches the 260 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real personal income confirms broad-based economic strength that supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS produces a negative predictive accuracy for 20-day forward returns during mid-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity that drags on economic growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Declining HOUST values fail to predict negative 60-day forward returns during late-cycle contractions, with a hit rate below 45 percent.
